sentence: كِيف حَالَك يَا شَيخ؟ اِنْتَ كُوَيِّس الْيَوم وَلَّا لَأ؟
raw: كيف حالك يا شيخ؟ انت كويس اليوم ولا لأ؟
ipa: ˈkiːf ˈħaːlak jaː ˈʃeːx ˈʔinta kuˈwajjis alˈjoːm ˈwallaː ˈlaʔa
gloss_en: How are you, sheikh? Are you well today or not?
notes: kiif haalak greeting (omar1975); ay→eː in sheekh; walla la' tag question. Row corrected: earlier izzayyak/innaharda were Cairene lexicon, not Hejazi

sentence: اَلْجَوّ فِي جِدَّة حَارّ وَرَطِب طُول السَّنَة.
raw: الجو في جدة حار ورطب طول السنة.
ipa: alˈdʒaww fiː ˈdʒidda ˈħaːrr ˈwarɑtˤib ˈtˤuːl ˈassana
gloss_en: The weather in Jeddah is hot and humid all year.
notes: ج=/dʒ/ ǧidda (abdoh2010); geminates وّ دّ; emphatic ط

sentence: مَا أَبْغَى قَهْوَة دَحِّين، أَبْغَى شَاهِي بِالنَّعْنَاع.
raw: ما أبغى قهوة دحين، أبغى شاهي بالنعناع.
ipa: maː ˈʔabɣaː ˈɡahwa daħˈħiːn ˈʔabɣaː ˈʃaːhiː binnaʕˈnaːʕ
gloss_en: I don't want coffee now, I want mint tea.
notes: abgha 'want'; dahhiin 'now' — the Hejazi form (abdoh2010), replacing the earlier Cairene dilwaqti; qaf→g in gahwa

sentence: اِشْتَرَيت تَلَات كَيلُو تَمَر هِنْدِي مِن السُّوق بِعِشْرِين رِيَال.
raw: اشتريت تلات كيلو تمر هندي من السوق بعشرين ريال.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈreːt taˈlaːt ˈkeːluː ˈtamar ˈhindiː min asˈsuːɡ biʕiʃˈriːn riˈjaːl
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of tamarind from the market for twenty riyals.
notes: ث→/t/ talāt (omar1975: urban Hejazi stop reflexes); ق=/ɡ/ sūg

sentence: هَادَا الظَّابِط رِجِع مِن الدَّوَام مَبْسُوط.
raw: هادا الظابط رجع من الدوام مبسوط.
ipa: ˈhaːdaː dˤˈdˤɑːbitˤ ˈridʒiʕ min addaˈwaːm mabˈsuːtˤ
gloss_en: This officer came back from work happy.
notes: hāda with ذ→/d/ (urban Hejazi, omar1975); ظ→/dˤ/ stop (abdoh2010); sun assimilation ad-d

sentence: تَعَال شُوف الْبَيْت الْجَدِيد وَقُول لِي رَايَك.
raw: تعال شوف البيت الجديد وقول لي رايك.
ipa: taˈʕaːl ˈʃuːf alˈbeːt aldʒaˈdiːd waˈɡuːl liː ˈraːjak
gloss_en: Come see the new house and tell me your opinion.
notes: Hejazi imperatives taʕal/shuf/gul; ay→ē monophthongization in albeːt (omar1975); qaf→g

gloss_en: The weather today isn't cold, it's nice.
notes: predicate negator مو /muː/ (Hejazi, omar1975), not Cairene mish; ħilu 'nice'; aw→oː in alyoːm; جو geminate ww

gloss_en: How much is this? Fifteen riyals.
notes: price question بِكَم bikam (omar1975); هَادَا hāda demonstrative (abdoh2010)

gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at four o'clock.
notes: aw→oː in mōʕid (omar1975); bukra 'tomorrow'; sun assimilation as-sāʕa

gloss_en: My family lives in Medina.
notes: ahl 'family', active participle sāknīn for present residence (omar1975)

gloss_en: Go straight, then turn right.
notes: imperatives rūḥ/lif; dughri 'straight' urban koine (omar1975); ay→eː in baʕdeːn

gloss_en: The food was very delicious.
notes: intensifier مَرَّة marra 'very' (Hejazi, omar1975); ذ→d ladīd urban stop reflex (abdoh2010); kān past

gloss_en: Where's the market? I don't know.
notes: interrogative fīn 'where' (omar1975); mā + imperfect negation; qaf→g sūg (abdoh2010)

gloss_en: How many brothers do you have?
notes: kam + singular count noun (omar1975); ʕindak possessive

gloss_en: If you want, we'll go together.
notes: conditional إذا; abغā 'want' Hejazi verb tabghā (omar1975); sawa 'together'; ذ→d in idā (abdoh2010)

gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the market and brought vegetables.
notes: past narrative ruḥt/jibt (jāb 'bring', omar1975); qaf→g sūg; emphatic ض in khudˤār

gloss_en: Tomorrow I'll travel to Mecca and Taif too.
notes: b-future prefix basāfir (omar1975); كَمَان kamān 'also' (Hejazi); sun assimilation aṭ-ṭāyif, emphatic ط

gloss_en: Call me on the mobile tomorrow.
notes: imperative kallim geminate ll; jawwāl 'mobile phone' جيم=/dʒ/ + geminate ww (abdoh2010)

gloss_en: Peace be upon you. And upon you peace.
notes: greeting adjacency pair; ay→eː in ʕaleːkum (omar1975); sun assimilation as-salām

gloss_en: May God give you good health.
notes: closing formula; emphatic ط in yaʕṭīk; sun assimilation al-ʕāfya; urban Hijazi emphatic/inventory phonology per (omar1975)
